milestone_label: #5-bootstrap correction-loop collector tool + first smoke against the wo_raw reverse-smoke project
status_note: Read-only psql-driven CLI (tools/bootstrap_correction_collector.py) that pulls unmatched detections from a project and emits a CSV+JSON correction worksheet with reviewer-fillable columns (corrected_label, corrected_owner_type, corrected_owner_id, suppress, reviewer, reviewed_at, notes). Skeleton for the >=50-correction accumulation gate that #5-bootstrap (retrain) is queued behind. Mutates nothing.

tool.interface: python3 tools/bootstrap_correction_collector.py [--project-id <uuid>] [--target-type {tool,face,device}] [--match-status {unmatched,low,matched}] [--min-score F] [--max-score F] [--limit N] [--out-csv PATH] [--out-json PATH] [--summary-only]
tool.data_source: bundled-pg via psql (no API token required); read-only joins on detections + photos + projects
tool.reviewer_workflow: emit -> human edits the seven trailing reviewer columns in CSV -> downstream importer (TBD, separate milestone) translates labelled rows into PATCH /api/projects/<pid>/recognition_items/<id>/correct calls so corrections show up in the existing recognition_items.corrected_owner_* columns and the v_training_corrections view that #7a retrain consumes
tool.safety: read-only; no DB mutations; idempotent re-run

reconciliation.notes: detection_count + distinct_photos + per_photo + score quantiles all match docs/baselines/2c-asia.json tool_false_positive_finding exactly (214 / 119 / 1.7983 / median 0.8906 / p75 0.9116 / max 0.9505).

new_finding_class_id_breakdown.context: #2c-asia recorded the per-photo tool FP rate and score quantiles, but did NOT break the 214 false positives down by COCO class_id. The collector smoke surfaces this for the first time.

0.label: person
0.interpretation: YOLOv8 fires class=person on every single ID photo (1 / photo, 119 / 119 cohort) -- the subject's own torso is being labelled person but emitted on the tool detection path because target_type for class 0..79 is hard-coded to 'tool' on the wo_raw recognition path (#5-skel scope revision).

27.label: tie
27.interpretation: YOLOv8 fires class=tie on 95 / 119 ID photos -- consistent with the #2c-asia visual hypothesis that uniform collars / lanyards / employee badges read as 'tie' to a COCO-trained YOLOv8n.

new_finding_class_id_breakdown.actionable: Fixes the score-threshold filter dead-end called out in #2c-asia tool_false_positive_finding: a class-specific blacklist that drops {person, tie} on the tool detection path would clear 214 / 214 = 100 % of these false positives on this cohort with no recall risk on the actual #5-bootstrap target classes (real worksite tools). Class 27 alone cuts 95 / 214 (44.4 %) without touching person.
routes_to: future class-specific suppression in worker/mod.rs run_real_pipeline (server Rust -- not in scope of this milestone)

non_goals: This milestone does NOT yet ship a labelled-corrections importer back into recognition_items.
This milestone does NOT yet feed #7a retrain (no corrections accumulated; gate is >=50).
This milestone does NOT modify server Rust or the wo_raw recognition path (per project rules).
This milestone does NOT draw bboxes onto images.
next_steps: Have a human reviewer label 50+ rows from /tmp/corrections-*.csv against the existing class_id breakdown (person + tie are both pure FPs on the ID-photo cohort).

Add a small importer (separate milestone) that reads the labelled CSV and posts PATCH /api/projects/<pid>/recognition_items/<id>/correct so corrections land in recognition_items.corrected_owner_* (and therefore v_training_corrections / #7a retrain).
Once >=50 corrections are recorded, exercise the existing #7a -> #7b-train -> #7c-skel/#7c-eval pipeline end-to-end -- that closes #5-bootstrap.
